semantic_journey: Arabic khaza'al = lameness, halting limping gait. Greek scholaios = leisurely, slow, unhurried (from skhole = leisure). Lameness is an involuntary physical defect; leisureliness is a voluntary chosen pace. No 2-step chain connects involuntary disability to deliberate unhurriedness.
key_insight: Lameness (involuntary disability) and leisureliness (voluntary pace) are semantically distinct despite superficially similar slowness.

semantic_journey: Arabic khazaqa = to pierce, impale, split. Greek andikha = asunder, in twain (spatial adverb from anti+dikha). Piercing is an action verb; andikha is a directional adverb morphologically built from Greek components. No semantic path connects the act of piercing to a spatial adverb for separation.
key_insight: Greek andikha is a morphological Greek compound; piercing-action and asunder-adverb occupy entirely different semantic fields.

semantic_journey: Arabic da'afa = to finish off, dispatch a wounded enemy, kill swiftly. Greek dapto = to devour, tear and consume as wild beasts do. Both encode lethal finalizing act on a victim. Arabic keeps human-executioner frame; Greek extends to beast-consumption. 1-step drift: kill-to-finish -> devour-to-consume.
key_insight: Arabic dispatching a fallen enemy and Greek beast-devouring share the lethal-consumption concept applied to victim or prey.

semantic_journey: Arabic khasaqa = to sink into, cause ground to open under pressure (hoof on earth). Greek khasko = to gape open. Core concept: forced aperture. Arabic encodes the forcing action; Greek encodes the resulting open state. 1-step action-to-result.
key_insight: Arabic ground-opening-under-force and Greek gaping-open share the concept of forced aperture as action then result.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-a'fath = a man much exposed or uncovered (nakedness). Greek leiphtheihe = be left behind (optative passive of leipo = to leave). Physical exposure and spatial abandonment are unrelated; no 2-step chain connects being uncovered to being abandoned.
key_insight: Exposed/uncovered (nakedness) and left-behind (abandoned) are unrelated semantic fields with no plausible 2-step path.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-thantal = spoiling and becoming foul after cleansing. Greek kataluthen = having been dissolved/undone (aorist passive of kataluo). Putrefaction (quality change: pure to foul) is distinct from structural dissolution (breaking down form). Connecting them requires 3+ steps.
key_insight: Putrefaction and structural dissolution are distinct processes; the semantic chain exceeds 2 steps.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-faqiy primarily means religious jurist/scholar (faqih). Original reasoning invoked maharith (plow instruments) as secondary gloss. Greek glupho = to carve, engrave. Even accepting plow-cutting, plowing earth and carving stone/wood are different actions on different materials for different purposes.
key_insight: Al-faqiy primarily means religious scholar; even the plow-cutting secondary sense does not match Greek stone/wood carving.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-rafaha = ease, luxury, comfort, refined living. Greek haliporpuros = sea-purple dyed. Chain: ease -> luxury -> luxury color -> purple requires 3+ steps of cultural association, not lexical semantics. No lexical path.
key_insight: Comfort/ease and sea-purple share only the cultural association that purple is luxurious, not any lexical semantic descent.
